name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value greater than the original input

name: align_result_not_multiple
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_multiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FF00,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of floor-alignment must always be a multiple of the alignment value

name: aligned_identity_broken
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_identity_broken(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning a value that is already aligned should be the identity operation

name: aligned_always_true
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_always_true(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 1,
        addr > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, size),
{
}
reason: Not every nonzero address is aligned to every size; alignment should be selective

name: align_not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FF00,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(align_to_usize(a, b), b) != align_to_usize(a, b),
{
}
reason: Floor-alignment must be idempotent — applying it twice should equal applying it once
